You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me understand the level layout better
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Categorize all objects
objects = {}
for n in nodes:
    c = n['color']
    if c not in objects:
        objects[c] = []
    objects[c].append(n)

print("Objects by color:")
for c, objs in sorted(objects.items()):
    total_pixels = sum(o['pixels'] for o in objs)
    print(f"  {c}: {len(objs)} objects, total pixels={total_pixels}")
    for o in objs[:3]:  # Show first 3 of each
        rows = [p[0] for p in o['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in o['boundary']]
        print(f"    id={o['id']}: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={o['pixels']}")

# Check agent position and nearby navigable space
agent = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b'][0]
agent_id = agent['id']

# Find what the agent is standing on / adjacent to
adj = seg['adjacency_list']
agent_neighbors = []
for a, b in adj:
    if a == agent_id or b == agent_id:
        other = b if a == agent_id else a
        for n in nodes:
            if n['id'] == other:
                agent_neighbors.append(n)
                break

print(f"\nAgent neighbors:")
for nb in agent_neighbors:
    rows = [p[0] for p in nb['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in nb['boundary']]
    print(f"  color={nb['color']}, pixels={nb['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
